item.description: The U.S. Secret Service shot and killed a person who opened fire at a security checkpoint in an exchange of gunfire that briefly locked down the White House, officials said. NBC News correspondents have the latest on the shooting.

media:content.media:title: Special Report: Secret Service kill man who opened fire outside of White House
media:content.media:description: The U.S. Secret Service shot and killed a person who opened fire at a security checkpoint in an exchange of gunfire that briefly locked down the White House, officials said. NBC News correspondents have the latest on the shooting.

item.title: Special Report: Secret Service kill man who opened fire outside of White House

item.description: Officials from the U.S. and Iran appear to be nearing a deal to end the ongoing Middle-East conflict, according to public statements from President Donald Trump Saturday. “An Agreement has been largely negotiated, subject to finalization between the United States of America, the Islamic Republic of Iran, and the various other Countries, as listed,” Trump wrote in a Truth Social post Saturday afternoon. NBC News' Raf Sanchez reports.

media:content.media:title: Trump says agreement on Iran war ‘largely negotiated’
media:content.media:description: Officials from the U.S. and Iran appear to be nearing a deal to end the ongoing Middle-East conflict, according to public statements from President Donald Trump Saturday. “An Agreement has been largely negotiated, subject to finalization between the United States of America, the Islamic Republic of Iran, and the various other Countries, as listed,” Trump wrote in a Truth Social post Saturday afternoon. NBC News' Raf Sanchez reports.

item.title: Trump says agreement on Iran war ‘largely negotiated’

item.description: Gov. Gavin Newsom (D-CA) declared a state of emergency in California on Saturday as thousands of people were forced to evacuate their homes over a toxic chemical tank that, authorities say, will either leak or explode. NBC News' Steve Patterson is on the ground just outside the evacuation zone.

media:content.media:title: State of emergency declared over southern California chemical incident
media:content.media:description: Gov. Gavin Newsom (D-CA) declared a state of emergency in California on Saturday as thousands of people were forced to evacuate their homes over a toxic chemical tank that, authorities say, will either leak or explode. NBC News' Steve Patterson is on the ground just outside the evacuation zone.

item.title: State of emergency declared over southern California chemical incident

item.dc:creator: Alexandra Marquez
item.description: The now-viral, AI-generated video opens with the iconic Hollywood sign on fire and features Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass wearing makeup to look like the Joker.

media:content.media:text: Spencer Pratt, Los Angeles mayor Karen Bass and Nithya Raman.
media:content.media:description: Spencer Pratt, Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass and Nithya Raman.

item.title: Reality check: Spencer Pratt seeks to convert online attention to real-life votes in L.A.

item.dc:creator: Marlene Lenthang
item.description: Three Red Cross volunteers have died, believed to have contracted the Ebola virus during a humanitarian mission in the Democratic Republic of the Congo in March, the organization said.“At the time of the intervention, the community was not aware of the Ebola Virus Disease outbreak, and the outbreak had not yet been identified.

media:content.media:text: Health Officials Race To Contain Deadly Ebola Outbreak In Congo
media:content.media:description: Health workers in protective equipment amid an Ebola outbreak in Mongbwalu, Democratic Republic of the Congo, on Thursday.

media:content.media:text: Health workers in gear.
media:content.media:description: Health workers in protective equipment amid an Ebola outbreak in Mongbwalu, Democratic Republic of the Congo, on Thursday.

media:content.media:text: TOPSHOT-DRCONGO-HEALTH-VIRUS-EBOLA
media:content.media:description: Health workers in protective equipment amid an Ebola outbreak in Mongbwalu, Democratic Republic of the Congo, on Thursday.

item.title: 3 Red Cross volunteers die of Ebola as number of cases rise in Uganda

item.dc:creator: Harry Rabinowitz
item.description: Shop the best Memorial Day deals on mattresses, home appliances, beauty, tech and more from brands like Brooklinen, Ninja, Levoit, EltaMD and Apple.

media:content.media:credit: Amazon; Kara Birnbaum / NBC News; Brooklinen
media:content.media:text: It may not be Black Friday, but Memorial Day weekend is one of the best times to shop before summer hits.
media:content.media:description: It may not be Black Friday, but Memorial Day weekend is one of the best times to shop before summer hits.

media:content.media:text: It may not be Black Friday, but Memorial Day weekend is one of the best times to shop before summer hits.
media:content.media:description: It may not be Black Friday, but Memorial Day weekend is one of the best times to shop before summer hits.

item.title: The 93+ absolute best Memorial Day sales worth shopping

item.description: On the sidelines of the Trump-Xi Summit in Beijing, NBC News' Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio to discuss China policy, the war with Iran, the future of Cuba, and more.

media:content.media:title: Extended Interview: Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio
media:content.media:description: On the sidelines of the Trump-Xi Summit in Beijing, NBC News' Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio to discuss China policy, the war with Iran, the future of Cuba, and more.

item.title: Extended Interview: Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio

item.dc:creator: Alexandra Marquez
item.description: The now-viral, AI-generated video opens with the iconic Hollywood sign on fire and features Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass wearing makeup to look like the Joker.

media:content.media:description: Spencer Pratt, Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass and Nithya Raman.

item.title: Reality check: Spencer Pratt seeks to convert online attention to real-life votes in L.A.

item.dc:creator: Kyla Guilfoil
item.description: The shooting at the White House Saturday evening is just the latest act of political violence carried out in President Donald Trump’s orbit in recent weeks.

media:content.media:text: Three secret service agents wearing black uniforms on the White House lawn at dusk.
media:content.media:description: The acts of political violence tied to the president come as politically-motivated attacks have escalated nationwide.

item.title: White House shooting marks another incident in a string of political violence

item.dc:creator: Steve Patterson
Dennis Romero
item.description: Firefighters on Saturday were trying to cool a chemical tank “literally on the edge” of exploding in Garden Grove, California, as they worked to avert a disastrous “worst-case” scenario, the incident’s commander said.

media:content.media:text: Spraying water and mist on a large chemical tank at dusk.
media:content.media:description: Water is sprayed on a tank that overheated at an aerospace plant in Garden Grove, Calif., May 22, 2026.

media:content.media:text: A firefighter in a red truck leans out of the driver's window and points.
media:content.media:description: An Orange County Fire Authority firefighter instructs a photographer to evacuate from the GKN Aerospace area in Garden Grove, Calif. on Friday.

media:content.media:text: A large american flag hanging on the wall of a makeshift shelter in a large white hangar.
media:content.media:description: An emergency evacuation center at Freedom Hall for residents near the area of the chemical leak at GKN Aerospace Transparency building, in Fountain Valley, Calif. on Saturday.

item.title: Chemical tank ‘on the edge’ of exploding in California as crews try to avert potential disaster

item.dc:creator: Julie Tsirkin
Megan Shannon
Tom Winter
Jonathan Dienst
Marlene Lenthang
item.description: The White House is on lockdown after dozens of gunshots were heard outside on Saturday evening.

media:content.media:text: An ambulance and other law enforcement vehicles parked with their lights flashing on a wet city street.
media:content.media:description: Emergency and police vehicles are seen near the White House in Washington, DC, on May 23, 2026.

media:content.media:text: Yellow Crime scene tape stretches across the frame in front of police officers.
media:content.media:description: A police cordon outside the White House complex on Saturday.

media:content.media:text: Three secret service agents wearing black uniforms on the White House lawn at dusk.
media:content.media:description: Secret Service agents patrolling the White House lawn.

media:content.media:text: A secret service agent with binoculars on the roof of a building at the White House.
media:content.media:description: Law enforcement officials survey from the rooftop of a building at the White House after shots were fired on Saturday.

item.title: Officials identify man killed by Secret Service after opening fire near White House

item.dc:creator: Marlene Lenthang
item.description: Three Red Cross volunteers have died, believed to have contracted the Ebola virus during a humanitarian mission in the Democratic Republic of the Congo in March, the organization said.“At the time of the intervention, the community was not aware of the Ebola Virus Disease outbreak, and the outbreak had not yet been identified.

item.dc:creator: Francie Ebert
item.description: Pop star Shakira on Saturday posted the official music video for “Dai Dai,” the 2026 World Cup anthem featuring Burna Boy, weeks before the FIFA tournament kicks off in North America

media:content.media:text: Shakira stands in front of a backdrop promoting the FIFA World Cup 2026.
media:content.media:description: Shakira was announced this month as a FIFA World Cup final halftime show performer.

item.title: Shakira’s World Cup anthem gets star-studded video treatment

item.dc:creator: Mirna Alsharif
Meriam Bouarrouj
item.description: An Illinois school shooting plot was foiled Friday after a relative spotted a boy with a firearm and called 911, according to the Will County Sheriff’s Office

media:content.media:text: Grand Prairie Elementary School.
media:content.media:description: Grand Prairie Elementary School.

item.title: Illinois school shooting plot foiled by relative who tackled the armed child, officials say

Keir Simmons

Kyla Guilfoil
item.description: Officials from the U.S. and Iran appear to be nearing a deal to end the ongoing Middle East conflict, according to public statements from President Donald Trump and officials from both nations Saturday

media:content.media:text: President Donald Trump in the Oval Office on May 21, 2026.
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump in the Oval Office on May 21, 2026.

item.title: Agreement on Iran war ‘largely negotiated,’ Trump says amid fragile ceasefire

item.dc:creator: Orlando Matos
Carmen Sesin
item.description: Some Cubans who’ve been able to buy solar panels say it’s a game changer, but most rely on other alternatives including charcoal, despite health concerns and the steep cost.

media:content.media:text: Solar panel specialists lift a panel onto the rooftop of a multi-family building in Matanzas, Cuba, on April 13, 2026.
media:content.media:description: Solar panel specialists lift a panel onto the rooftop of a multi-family building in Matanzas, Cuba, on April 13.

media:content.media:text: Cuba Self Sufficient
media:content.media:description: Solar panel specialists lift a panel onto the rooftop of a multi-family building in Matanzas, Cuba, on April 13.

media:content.media:text: cooking with charcoal in Havana, Cuba
media:content.media:description: Solar panel specialists lift a panel onto the rooftop of a multi-family building in Matanzas, Cuba, on April 13.

media:content.media:text: A woman cooks with charcoal at her home in Yaguaramas, Cienfuegos Province, Cuba, March 20, 2025.
media:content.media:description: Solar panel specialists lift a panel onto the rooftop of a multi-family building in Matanzas, Cuba, on April 13.

media:content.media:text: A solar panel on a residential building in Matanzas, Cuba, on April 13, 2026.
media:content.media:description: Solar panel specialists lift a panel onto the rooftop of a multi-family building in Matanzas, Cuba, on April 13.

item.title: From solar to charcoal, Cubans look for alternatives amid outages and fuel shortages

item.dc:creator: Marlene Lenthang
Matt Lavietes
item.description: NASCAR legend Kyle Busch died of severe pneumonia, his family said Saturday

media:content.media:text: Kyle Busch during practice for the NASCAR Craftsman Truck Series ECOSAVE 200 at Dover Motor Speedway on May 15, 2026 in Dover, Del.
media:content.media:description: Kyle Busch during practice for the NASCAR Craftsman Truck Series ECOSAVE 200 at Dover Motor Speedway on May 15, 2026 in Dover, Del.

media:content.media:text: Kyle Busch embraces his wife, Samantha, and son, Brexton, prior to a race in 2022.
media:content.media:description: Kyle Busch embraces his wife, Samantha, and son, Brexton, prior to a race in 2022.

item.title: NASCAR legend Kyle Busch died of pneumonia-induced sepsis, his family says

item.dc:creator: Matt Lavietes
item.description: Stephen Colbert returns to late night TV a day after final taping of ‘Late Show’

media:content.media:text: The Late Show with Stephen Colbert.
media:content.media:description: Stephen Colbert on the CBS series The Late Show with Stephen Colbert

item.title: Stephen Colbert returns to late night a day after final taping of ‘Late Show’

item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
item.description: The estranged husband of a prominent New York City art dealer was convicted Friday of hiring a hitman to kill him in Brazil

media:content.media:text: Brent Sikkema.
media:content.media:description: Brent Sikkema on Sept. 11, 2007 in New York City.

item.title: Estranged husband convicted of murder-for-hire in killing of art dealer

item.dc:creator: Reuters
item.description: The death toll from a drone strike on a student dorm in the Russian-controlled Luhansk region of eastern Ukraine has risen to 16, with most of the victims young women, Russian officials said on Saturday, after a heated ⁠U.N.

media:content.media:text: Aftermath of drone attack on Luhansk college amid Russia-Ukraine conflict.
media:content.media:description: A destroyed building of the Starobilsk College of Luhansk Pedagogical University following a recent attack in the Luhansk region, a Russian-controlled area of Ukraine, on Saturday.

item.title: Russia blames Ukraine, vows retaliation as 16 killed in strike on student dorm

item.dc:creator: Alicia Victoria Lozano
Dennis Romero
item.description: SAN DIEGO — As an elementary school student at the Islamic Center of San Diego in the early aughts, Sarah Youssef said she doesn’t remember there being guards on patrol or gates keeping out danger

media:content.media:text: At Least 3 Killed In Shooting At Islamic Center of San Diego
media:content.media:description: Mosque members embrace in the parking lot of the Islamic Center of San Diego on Tuesday. Three people were killed and two suspects are dead after a shooting at San Diego's largest mosque.

media:content.media:text: US-RELIGION-CRIME
media:content.media:description: Mosque members embrace in the parking lot of the Islamic Center of San Diego on Tuesday. Three people were killed and two suspects are dead after a shooting at San Diego's largest mosque.

media:content.media:text: Several children, with adults in the background, at a candlelight vigil at dusk.
media:content.media:description: Mosque members embrace in the parking lot of the Islamic Center of San Diego on Tuesday. Three people were killed and two suspects are dead after a shooting at San Diego's largest mosque.

media:content.media:text: US-SHOOTING-CRIME-ISLAMIC CENTER
media:content.media:description: Mosque members embrace in the parking lot of the Islamic Center of San Diego on Tuesday. Three people were killed and two suspects are dead after a shooting at San Diego's largest mosque.

item.title: The deepest fears of San Diego’s religious communities were realized in mosque shooting

item.dc:creator: Minyvonne Burke
item.description: An elderly California couple found dead in a suspected murder-suicide had recently been scammed by someone posing as actor Tom Selleck, a longtime friend said

media:content.media:text: Donald Whitaker, 80, and his wife Karen Whitaker,
media:content.media:description: Donald and Karen Whitaker.

item.title: Woman dead in murder-suicide may have been victim of a celebrity fraud scheme
